items.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Pre-Auth RCE (CVSS 9.1) Actively Exploited — EKZ Infostealer Deployed
items.summary: A critical pre-authentication API bypass (CVSS 9.1) in Fortinet FortiClient EMS 7.4.5–7.4.6 allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary commands without any credentials. Threat actors are actively exploiting it to deploy the EKZ Infostealer — disguised as a legitimate Fortinet patch — to steal Chrome and Firefox credentials including bypassing Chrome's encrypted password storage.
items.why: CISA added this to its KEV catalog (April 6, 2026) with an emergency federal remediation deadline. The zero-click pre-auth exploitation path combined with an active credential-theft campaign targeting enterprise endpoints makes this critical for any organization running FortiClient EMS.

items.title: CVE-2025-48595: Android Framework Zero-Day (CVSS 8.4) Actively Exploited — Patched June 2, 2026
items.summary: Google's June 2026 Android security bulletin (124 CVEs) patches an actively exploited zero-day in the Android Framework — CVE-2025-48595 (CVSS 8.4). The integer overflow flaw enables local privilege escalation with no user interaction across Android 14, 15, 16, and 16-QRP2. Google attributes exploitation to likely commercial spyware or nation-state actors targeting high-value individuals.
items.why: No-interaction-required privilege escalation with confirmed in-the-wild exploitation is a hallmark of targeted spyware (e.g., Pegasus-style operations). All Android 14+ devices remain vulnerable until updated to the June 5 2026 patch level.

items.title: APT28 Operation Neusploit: MS Office CVE-2026-21509 Weaponized Within 24 Hours of Disclosure
items.summary: Russian state-sponsored APT28 (Fancy Bear) exploited a newly disclosed Microsoft Office OLE bypass (CVE-2026-21509) within 24 hours of its January 26 disclosure, targeting European military, government, and maritime organizations across Poland, Ukraine, Greece, UAE, and Slovenia. The multi-stage chain deploys an Outlook VBA backdoor (NotDoor) and a modified Covenant C2 implant, routing all traffic through the legitimate cloud storage service filen.io to blend with normal activity.
items.why: APT28's 24-hour weaponization window and cloud-C2 blending technique eliminate traditional IOC-based detection. Any unpatched Office installation across government, defense, or maritime sectors is an active target for ongoing espionage.
items.source: The Hacker News / Trellix / Zscaler ThreatLabz

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack: 30+ Red Hat @redhat-cloud-services npm Packages Backdoored on June 1, 2026
items.summary: Attackers compromised a Red Hat employee's GitHub account on June 1, 2026, injecting malicious GitHub Actions workflows into three RedHatInsights repositories to backdoor 30+ packages in the @redhat-cloud-services npm scope (~80,000 weekly downloads). The Miasma payload executes automatically on every npm install — before application code runs — and sweeps AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, HashiCorp Vault, GitHub Actions, npm, and CircleCI secrets.
items.why: Executes at install time making it invisible to most app-layer security tools. Targets CI/CD pipeline secrets enabling full lateral movement across multi-cloud infrastructure. Any pipeline running npm install against these packages on June 1–2 should be treated as fully compromised and all cloud tokens rotated immediately.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: AI Slashes eCrime Breakout Time to 29 Minutes, Attacks Surge 89%
items.summary: CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report reveals AI-enabled attacks surged 89% YoY. Average eCrime lateral movement breakout time collapsed to 29 minutes (fastest ever recorded: 27 seconds; data exfiltration began 4 minutes after initial access in one case). Russia-nexus FANCY BEAR deployed LLM-enabled malware (LAMEHUG) for automated reconnaissance; DPRK FAMOUS CHOLLIMA used AI-generated personas for insider threat operations. Adversaries are also prompt-injecting GenAI tools at 90+ organizations.
items.why: A 29-minute breakout window eliminates human-speed SOC response for most organizations. With AI now acting as a force multiplier from eCrime to nation-state, defenders must automate detection and containment — traditional 1-hour SLAs for triage are functionally obsolete.
